front: Play: Lady Bracknell exclaims 'a handbag?!' when questioning her prospective son-in-law about his upbringing
back: The Importance of Being Earnest (Oscar Wilde)

front: Play: Jack proves he was abandoned as a baby at Victoria station and has the necessary name to marry Gwendolen Fairfax; Miss Prism swapped the baby
back: The Importance of Being Earnest (Oscar Wilde)

front: Novel: the preface declares 'All art is quite useless' and compares dislike of Realism and Romanticism to 'different rages of Caliban'
back: The Picture of Dorian Gray (Oscar Wilde)

front: Novel: Basil Hallward paints a portrait that ages instead of the title character, who reads a 'yellow book' from Lord Henry Wotton
back: The Picture of Dorian Gray (Oscar Wilde)

front: Novel: Albert Lewin's film adaptation uses Technicolor only for Ivan Albright's grotesque painting of the title object
back: The Picture of Dorian Gray (Oscar Wilde)

front: Play: Mrs. Erlynne claims she accidentally took the title object to create a distraction in Lord Darlington's rooms; Lord Darlington says 'we are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars'
Play: a government minister who made his fortune from insider trading is blackmailed into supporting a fraudulent Argentine canal scheme
Play: written in French for Sarah Bernhardt; Aubrey Beardsley illustrated the English version after liking a drawing of a woman kissing a severed head
Poem: 'the coward does it with a kiss / The brave man with a sword!' — declares 'each man kills the thing he loves'
work: "The Ballad of Reading Gaol" and Prison Writings

front: Author: De Profundis was a prison letter to 'Bosie' asserting 'Christ's place indeed is with the poets'; adopted the pseudonym Sebastian Melmoth after release
work: "The Ballad of Reading Gaol" and Prison Writings

front: Author: tomb at Pere Lachaise designed by Jacob Epstein with a sphinxlike winged angel; glass barrier installed to prevent lipstick damage
work: "The Ballad of Reading Gaol" and Prison Writings

front: Short Story: a swallow planning to go to Egypt distributes a statue's jewels to the poor
Short Story: an American family uses Pinkerton's Champion Stain Remover to remove ghostly blood stains
Short Story: a man tries to kill his uncle with an exploding Liberty-figurine clock to fulfill a prophecy by the palm reader Septimus Podgers
Author: 'Life imitates art far more than Art imitates life' — Vivian argues in an essay while Cyril listens
Author: became a US celebrity through an 1882 lecture tour so Americans could compare him to Reginald Bunthorne from Gilbert and Sullivan's Patience; photographed by Napoleon Sarony
